name: Confederate States of America (Third National Flag, 1865)

colors.symbolism: the vertical fly-side bar — added so the Stainless Banner would not be confused for a flag of truce when limp
the saltire of the battle flag in the canton

description: A white field with a vertical red bar at the fly side, bearing in the upper hoist the square Confederate Battle Flag canton (a red field with a blue saltire fimbriated white bearing thirteen white stars).
history: Also called the "Blood-Stained Banner". Adopted by the Confederate Congress on 4 March 1865 — the Flag Act of 1865 — to address the persistent complaint that the Stainless Banner of 1863 looked like a flag of truce when hanging limp. The fix was simply to add a vertical red bar at the fly. The flag was in use only for about a month before the surrender of the Army of Northern Virginia at Appomattox on 9 April 1865 ended the Confederacy's effective existence; little of it was actually produced.

features.symbolism: a vertical red bar on the fly added by the Flag Act of 1865 so that the flag would not be mistaken for a flag of truce when hanging limp — the addition that gave it the nickname "Blood-Stained Banner"

features.role: Confederate Battle Flag as a square canton
features.symbolism: the same square Confederate Battle Flag canton carried over from the Stainless Banner — a red field with a blue saltire fimbriated white bearing thirteen white five-pointed stars
the Saint Andrew's saltire of the Southern Cross — a blue saltire fimbriated white on the red battle-flag field
thirteen white stars on the saltire — the eleven seceding states plus Missouri and Kentucky
